questions.question: In which month is the chapter 'Industrial Revolution' scheduled for study?
questions.explanation: The syllabus lists Industrial Revolution under January.

questions.question: True or False: 'Road safety' is a topic included in the Geography unit for Class 9.
questions.explanation: Road safety belongs to the Civics unit, not Geography.

questions.question: "Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime" என்ற தலைப்பில் எந்த அலகு உள்ளது?
options: Unit 1: Evolution of Humans and Society
Unit 4: Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime
Unit 7: State and Society in Medieval India
questions.explanation: இந்த தலைப்பு அலகு 4-இல் உள்ளது.

questions.question: ‘Early Tamil Society and Culture’ பாடத்தில், பண்டைய தமிழர்களின் சமூக அமைப்பை விவரிக்கும் பின்வரும் கூற்று எது சரியானது?
options: சமுதாயம் மூன்று முக்கிய வர்க்கங்களாக – இராஜா, வணிகர், விவசாயி – பிரிக்கப்பட்டது.
சமுதாயம் மூன்று முக்கிய வர்க்கங்களாக – வம்சம், சாமியார், தொழிலாளர் – பிரிக்கப்பட்டது.
சமுதாயம் இரண்டு முக்கிய வர்க்கங்களாக – ஆட்சி வர்க்கம் (குலம்) மற்றும் தொழில் வர்க்கம் (சாமியார்) – பிரிக்கப்பட்டது.
சமுதாயம் பல்வேறு குலங்களாகவும், ஒவ்வொரு குலத்திலும் பல தொழில்கள் இருந்தன; ஒற்றை வர்க்கம் இல்லை.
questions.explanation: புத்தகத்தில் early Tamil society பல்வேறு குலங்களாகவும், ஒவ்வொரு குலத்திலும் பல்வேறு தொழில்கள் இருந்தன; குறிப்பிட்ட வர்க்க அமைப்பு இல்லை என்று குறிப்பிடுகிறது.

questions.question: சமச்சீர்க் கல்வி புத்தகத்தின் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ அத்தியாயத்தில், அரசு எத்தகைய தொலைபேசி எண்களை குழந்தைகளுக்கு உதவியாக வழங்குகிறது?
questions.explanation: புத்தகத்தில் 10981098 மற்றும் 1441714417 என்ற இரு எண்களும் குழந்தைகளுக்கான உதவிக்கு குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளன.

questions.question: கீழ்காணும் அறிக்கைகளில், ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ அத்தியாயத்தில் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளவற்றில் எது தவறானது?
options: குழந்தைகள் 18 வயதுக்கு கீழ் என்றால் உதவி கிடைக்கும்

questions.question: ‘Early Tamil Society and Culture’ அத்தியாயத்தில், பண்டைய தமிழர்களின் முதன்மை வாழ்வாதாரமாக இருந்தது எது?
questions.explanation: அத்தியாயம் குறிப்பிடும் படி, ஆரம்ப கால தமிழர்கள் பெரும்பாலும் விவசாயத்தை தங்கள் முதன்மை வாழ்வாதாரமாகக் கொண்டிருந்தனர்.

questions.question: ‘State and Society in Medieval India’ அத்தியாயத்தின் படி, 13ஆம் நூற்றாண்டில் தமிழ்நாட்டில் நிறுவப்பட்ட முதன்மை அரசியல் அமைப்பு எது?
questions.explanation: 13ஆம் நூற்றாண்டில் சோழப் பேரரசு தமிழ்நாட்டில் மிகுந்த செல்வாக்கை பெற்றது.

questions.question: ‘Human Rights’ civics அத்தியாயத்தின் படி, கீழ்க்கண்டவற்றில் மனித உரிமை பிரகடனம் (UDHR) இல் சேராதது எது?
questions.explanation: UDHR இல் மதச் சுதந்திரம் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டாலும், குறிப்பாக 'மதம்' என்ற சொல் உரிமை பட்டியலில் இல்லை.

questions.question: ‘Forms of Government and Democracy’ அத்தியாயத்தில், ‘மக்களாட்சி’ (Democracy) மற்றும் ‘அரசு-நீதித்துறை-பொது (Republic)’ இடையிலான வேறுபாட்டை சரியாக விளக்குவது எது?
options: மக்களாட்சி என்பது ஜனநாயகத்தின் வடிவம், குடியரசு என்பது தலைவரின் தேர்வு முறையை குறிக்கும்.
மக்களாட்சி என்பது ஒருதலைப்பட்ச ஆட்சி, குடியரசு பலதலைப்பட்சம்.
மக்களாட்சி என்பது சட்டத்தின் மீது அடிப்படையில்லாதது, குடியரசு சட்டத்தின் மேல்.
மக்களாட்சி என்பது அரசின் எல்லைகளை நிர்ணயிக்கும், குடியரசு பொருளாதாரத்தை நிர்ணயிக்கும்.
questions.explanation: மக்களாட்சி ஜனநாயகத்தின் ஒரு வடிவம்; குடியரசு தலைவரின் தேர்வு முறையை (மதிப்பீடு) குறிக்கும்.

questions.question: ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ பகுதியில், ‘அநீதியற்ற சமூகங்கள்’ (Dalits) மீது விதிக்கப்பட்ட சட்டக் கட்டுப்பாடுகள் எவை?
questions.explanation: இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பு மற்றும் சிறப்பு சட்டங்கள் அநீதியற்ற சமூகங்களுக்கு பாதுகாப்பு வழங்குகின்றன.

questions.question: ‘Intellectual Awakening and Socio-Political Changes’ அத்தியாயத்தில், 19ஆம் நூற்றாண்டில் இந்தியாவில் சமூக சீர்திருத்த இயக்கத்தைத் தொடங்கியவர் யார்?
questions.explanation: ராம்முனி 19ஆம் நூற்றாண்டில் சமூக சீர்திருத்தம் மற்றும் கல்வி முன்னேற்றத்திற்கு பங்களித்தார்.

questions.question: எந்த ஆண்டு 'Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime' என்ற தலைப்பில் தமிழ்நாடு மாநில கல்வித்துறையின் சமூக அறிவியல் புத்தகத்தில் வெளியிடப்பட்டது?
questions.explanation: புத்தகம் 2018 ஆம் ஆண்டில் வெளியிடப்பட்டது.

questions.question: மூலப்பொருள்: ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ புத்தகத்தில் கூறப்படும் ‘குழந்தை திருமணம்’ குறித்த தொலைபேசி எண் எது?
questions.explanation: குழந்தை திருமணம் தொடர்பான உதவி தொலைபேசி எண் 10981098.

questions.question: புத்தகத்தின் படி, ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ என்ற தலைப்பில் உள்ள ‘வழிகாட்டுதல்’ சேவைகள் எத்தனை மணி நேரம் கிடைக்கின்றன?
questions.explanation: 24 × 7 மணி நேரம் கிடைக்கின்றன.

questions.question: புத்தகத்தில் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ என்ற தலைப்பின் கீழ் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ள ‘வழிகாட்டுதல்’ சேவையின் தொலைபேசி எண் எது?
புத்தகத்தில் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ என்ற தலைப்பில் உள்ள ‘வழிகாட்டுதல்’ சேவையின் நோக்கம் என்ன?
questions.explanation: சட்ட உதவிக்காக.

questions.question: புத்தகத்தின் படி, ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ புத்தகத்தின் வெளியீட்டு ஆண்டு எது?
questions.explanation: 2018 ஆம் ஆண்டு வெளியீடு.

questions.question: புத்தகத்தில் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ புத்தகத்தின் ‘Revised Edition’ எது?
questions.explanation: Revised Edition 2019.

questions.question: புத்தகத்தில் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ புத்தகத்தின் ‘Reprint’ ஆண்டு எது?
questions.explanation: Reprint 2021.

questions.question: புத்தகத்தில் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ புத்தகத்தில் ‘குழந்தை தொழிலாளர்’ மற்றும் ‘குழந்தை திருமணம்’ குறித்த உதவி சேவைகள் எந்த தொலைபேசி எண்ணில் கிடைக்கின்றன?
கொடுக்கப்பட்ட அட்டவணையில் 'Early Tamil Society and Culture' என்ற அத்தியாயம் எந்த மாதத்தில் வெளியிடப்பட்டது?
questions.explanation: அட்டவணை படி, 'Early Tamil Society and Culture' 33 பக்கம் ஜூலை மாதத்தில் உள்ளது.

questions.question: கீழ்காணும் அறிக்கைகளில் எது 'Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime' அத்தியாயத்தின் முக்கிய நோக்கத்தை சரியாக விவரிக்கிறது?
options: அதிகாரப்பூர்வமாக ஒழுங்கு செய்யப்படாத சமூகங்களைப் பாதுகாக்கும் சட்டங்களை அறிமுகப்படுத்துதல்
சமூகத்தில் ஒழுக்கமற்ற நடைமுறைகளை எதிர்க்கும் விழிப்புணர்வை ஏற்படுத்துதல்
questions.explanation: அத்தியாயம் ஒழுக்கமற்ற நடைமுறைகளான Untouchability-ஐ எதிர்க்கும் விழிப்புணர்வை உருவாக்குவதை நோக்கமாகக் கொண்டுள்ளது.

questions.question: கீழ்காணும் அறிக்கைகளில், ‘அன்டர்செட்டபிலிட்டி’ (Untouchability) பற்றிய சட்ட நிலையை சரியாக விவரிப்பது எது?
questions.explanation: புத்தகத்தில் ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ என்று குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளது, அதனால் அது குற்றமாகும் மற்றும் சட்டரீதியாக தடை செய்யப்பட்டுள்ளது.

questions.question: True or False: According to the Samacheer Kalvi chapter on Human Rights, the right to education is listed as a socio‑economic right, not a civil‑political right.
questions.explanation: The textbook classifies the right to education under socio‑economic rights, alongside health and work.

questions.question: True or False: According to the Samacheer chapter “The Classical World”, the Mauryan Empire under Ashoka adopted a policy of religious tolerance that included patronage of both Hindu temples and Buddhist monasteries.
questions.explanation: The textbook states that Ashoka’s edicts promoted dhamma and supported Buddhist monasteries while also respecting existing Hindu shrines.

questions.question: கீழ்க்காணும் அறிக்கைகளில், ‘அன்டச்சபிலிட்டி’ (Untouchability) பற்றிய சரியான விளக்கத்தைத் தேர்ந்தெடுக்கவும்.
options: அது சமூகத்தில் சமத்துவத்தை ஊக்குவிக்கும் ஒரு நடைமுறை.
அது மனித உரிமைகளுக்கு எதிரான ஒரு குற்றமாக கருதப்படுகிறது.
questions.explanation: அன்டச்சபிலிட்டி மனித உரிமைகளுக்கு எதிரான குற்றமாக கருதப்படுகிறது மற்றும் இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பில் தடைசெய்யப்பட்டுள்ளது.

questions.question: ‘Early Tamil Society and Culture’ பகுதியில், பண்டைய தமிழர் சமூக அமைப்பை விவரிக்கும் போது ‘சமுதாயம்’ என்ற சொல்லுக்கு கீழ் வரும்வற்றில் எது தவறானது?
questions.explanation: ‘Early Tamil Society and Culture’ பாடத்தில் பண்டைய தமிழர் சமூக அமைப்பு அரசியல், சங்கம், வணிகம் போன்றவற்றை உள்ளடக்கியது; ‘அரசு மற்றும் இராஜதந்திரம்’ என்பது வேறு காலத்தின் அம்சம்.

questions.question: ‘State and Society in Medieval India’ அத்தியாயத்தின் படி, 13ஆம் நூற்றாண்டில் ‘வெள்ளைத் துருவம்’ (Vijayanagara) பேரரசின் முக்கியமான நிர்வாகப் பணி எது?
options: நிலம் ஒதுக்கீடு (Land Grant) மற்றும் வரி வசூல்
questions.explanation: வெள்ளைத் துருவம் பேரரசில் நில ஒதுக்கீடு, வரி வசூல் போன்ற நிர்வாகப் பணிகள் முக்கியமாக இருந்தன; கடல் வணிகம், அச்சிடுதல் மற்றும் கோயில் கட்டுமானம் தனித்துவமான செயல்கள்.

questions.question: According to the chapter ‘Colonialism in Asia and Africa’, which of the following policies was NOT a feature of British rule in India?
questions.explanation: The Permanent Settlement was introduced by the British in Bengal, not in the Madras Presidency where the Ryotwari system was applied.

questions.question: கொடுக்கப்பட்ட ‘Untouchability is Inhuman and a Crime’ அத்தியாயத்தில், அரசு 10981098 என்ற தொலைபேசி எண் எந்த வகை உதவிக்காக வழங்கப்பட்டுள்ளது?
questions.explanation: அத்தியாயத்தில் 10981098 என்ற எண் குழந்தை திருமணம், குழந்தை தொழிலாளர், பாலியல் மற்றும் உடல் துன்புறுத்தல் போன்றவற்றுக்கு உதவிக்காக குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளது.

questions.question: பின்வரும் அறிக்கைகளில், ‘Intellectual Awakening and Socio-Political Changes’ அத்தியாயத்தில் குறிப்பிடப்பட்ட ‘மகாத்மா காந்தி’ அவர்களின் பங்களிப்பு குறித்து சரியானது எது?
options: அவர் இந்திய சுதந்திரப் போராட்டத்தில் முக்கிய பங்கு வகித்தார்.
questions.explanation: அத்தியாயம் மகாத்மா காந்தியின் சுதந்திரப் போராட்டத்தில் முக்கிய பங்களிப்பை மட்டுமே குறிப்பிடுகிறது.

questions.question: ‘Early Tamil Society and Culture’ பகுதியில், சங்க காலத்தில் திருவள்ளுவர் எழுதிய ‘திருக்குறள்’ முதன்மையாக எந்தத் துறையைச் சார்ந்தது?
questions.explanation: திருக்குறள் நெறிமுறை, நலன், நீதிமுறை ஆகியவற்றை மையமாகக் கொண்டது; இது சங்க காலத்தின் நெறி-நலக் கல்வியின் பிரதிபலிப்பு.

questions.question: ‘State and Society in Medieval India’ பகுதியில், 13ஆம் நூற்றாண்டில் புலவர் ‘கம்பன்’ எழுதிய ‘திருவள்ளுவர்’ (Kamba Ramayanam) இல், சமூக ஒழுங்கை உறுதிப்படுத்திய முக்கியக் கருத்து எது?
questions.explanation: கம்பனின் ‘திருவள்ளுவர்’ (Kamba Ramayanam) இல் விதி (Dharma) சமூக ஒழுங்கை நிலைநிறுத்தும் மையக் கருத்தாக உள்ளது.

questions.question: கீழ்க்காணும் அறிக்கைகளில், ‘அன்டிடிஸ்டிப்டபிலிட்டி’ (Untouchability) குறித்த சட்டப்பூர்வ நிலையை சரியாக விவரிப்பது எது?
options: அது ஒரு மனித உரிமை மீறல் மட்டுமே
அது 1950 ஆம் ஆண்டு சட்டத்தில் மட்டுமே குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ளது
அது 2020 ஆம் ஆண்டு திருத்தப்பட்ட சட்டத்தில் நீக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது
questions.explanation: அன்டிடிஸ்டிப்டபிலிட்டி இந்திய அரசியலமைப்பில் குற்றமாக அறிவிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது.

questions.question: ‘Early Tamil Society and Culture’ பகுதியில், சங்க காலத்தில் திருவள்ளுவர் எழுதிய ‘திருக்குறள்’ முதன்மையாக எந்த துறையில் சமூக நெறிகளை விளக்குகிறது?
questions.explanation: திருக்குறள் முதன்மையாக நெறிமுறை மற்றும் நெறி (அறம், பொருள், இன்பம்) பற்றிய போதனைகளை வழங்குகிறது.

questions.question: ‘Human Rights’ அத்தியாயத்தின் படி, கீழ்க்கண்ட உரைச்சொற்களில் எது ‘உயிர் உரிமை’ (Right to Life) என்பதின் விளக்கமாகும்?
options: எந்த மனிதனும் சட்டப்படி மரண தண்டனை பெற முடியாது.
எந்த மனிதனும் சுதந்திரமாக வேலை செய்ய உரிமை பெற்றவர்.
எந்த மனிதனும் தன்னுடைய உடல் மற்றும் மனநலத்தை பாதுகாக்க உரிமை பெற்றவர்.
எந்த மனிதனும் அரசியலமைப்பில் குறிப்பிடப்பட்டுள்ள அனைத்து உரிமைகளையும் பெற வேண்டும்.
questions.explanation: ‘உயிர் உரிமை’ என்பது தனிநபரின் உடல் மற்றும் மனநலத்தை பாதுகாக்கும் உரிமையை குறிக்கிறது.

questions.question: What is the primary occupation of most people involved in agriculture in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Most people involved in agriculture in Tamil Nadu are landless labourers.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true regarding land holdings in Tamil Nadu?
options: All landholders have large areas of land
Many landholders have very little land
Landholdings are uniform across the state
questions.explanation: Many landholders in Tamil Nadu have very little land.

questions.question: During 2015-16, the number of cultivators in Tamil Nadu was approximately 79.38 lakh. Five years earlier, it was about 81.18 lakh. What was the main trend observed?
questions.explanation: There was a decrease in the number of cultivators over five years.

questions.question: The area under cultivation in Tamil Nadu reduced from 64.88 lakh hectares to 59.71 lakh hectares during 2015-16. What does this indicate?
questions.explanation: The reduction indicates a decrease in cultivated land area.

questions.question: Most cultivators in Tamil Nadu are classified as _____ farmers.
questions.explanation: Most cultivators are micro farmers who cultivate small areas.

questions.question: What percentage of the total population of workers in Tamil Nadu was involved in agriculture according to the 2001 census?
questions.explanation: According to the 2001 census, 49.3% of workers were involved in agriculture.

questions.question: By 2011, the percentage of workers involved in agriculture in Tamil Nadu had decreased to _____.
questions.explanation: The percentage reduced to 42.1% in 2011.

questions.question: Approximately how many agricultural workers were there in Tamil Nadu in 2011?
questions.explanation: There were approximately 96 lakh agricultural workers in 2011.

questions.question: In 2011, what proportion of women were involved in agriculture in Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Nearly 55% of women were involved in agriculture in 2011.

questions.question: What percentage of males in Tamil Nadu were involved in agriculture in 2011?
questions.explanation: Approximately 35.3% of males were involved in agriculture.

questions.question: Assertion: The number of farmers in Tamil Nadu has increased in the last 10 years. Reason: Urbanization has led to more farmers.
options: Both assertion and reason are true and reason explains assertion
Both assertion and reason are true but reason does not explain assertion
Assertion is true but reason is false
Assertion is false but reason is true
questions.explanation: The number of farmers has decreased, not increased, so assertion is false.

questions.question: Assertion: Water and irrigation are important for agriculture in Tamil Nadu. Reason: Tamil Nadu has abundant rainfall throughout the year.
options: Both assertion and reason are true and reason explains assertion
Both assertion and reason are true but reason does not explain assertion
Assertion is true but reason is false
Assertion is false but reason is true
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu does not have abundant rainfall throughout the year; irrigation is essential.

questions.question: Assertion: Most farmers in Tamil Nadu cultivate in small areas. Reason: The landholdings are generally small.
options: Both assertion and reason are true and reason explains assertion
Both assertion and reason are true but reason does not explain assertion
Assertion is true but reason is false
Assertion is false but reason is true
questions.explanation: Both statements are true and related.

questions.question: True or False: The total area under cultivation in Tamil Nadu increased from 2010 to 2015.
questions.explanation: The area decreased during this period.

questions.question: True or False: The majority of agricultural workers in Tamil Nadu are women.
questions.explanation: Nearly 55% of women involved in agriculture indicates a majority.

questions.question: True or False: The number of cultivators in Tamil Nadu increased between 2010 and 2015.
questions.explanation: The number of cultivators decreased in this period.

questions.question: Match the following terms with their correct descriptions:
options: Workers with no land of their own
questions.explanation: Correct answer is Farmers cultivating small areas

questions.question: Which of the following crops is likely grown in Tamil Nadu based on its agriculture?
questions.explanation: Rice is a major crop in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key characteristic used to distinguish 'civilisation' from early forms of societies?
questions.explanation: Urban societies had social stratification and well-planned cities, which distinguishes them as civilisations.

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT mentioned as one of the important early civilisations?
questions.explanation: The text lists Egyptian, Mesopotamian, Chinese, and Indus as the important early civilisations.

questions.question: What emerged when it became necessary for large numbers of people to live in close proximity?
questions.explanation: Proximity of large populations led to the need for planning, organisation, and specialisation.

questions.question: The term 'civilisation' is used to describe an advanced, organised way of life that is an adaptation to:
questions.explanation: Civilisation is seen as an adaptation to particular environmental and cultural contexts.

questions.question: What happened to food and craft production as civilisations began to take shape?
questions.explanation: As civilisations took shape, food production and craft production were regulated.

questions.question: What did South India witness during the time of the early civilisations?
options: The rise of the Egyptian civilisation
Neolithic agro-pastoral communities and Microlithic life
The development of the Mesopotamian state
Urban planning similar to the Indus valley
questions.explanation: South India witnessed the emergence of Neolithic agro-pastoral communities and Microlithic form of life.

questions.question: The Egyptian civilisation is specifically known for its:
questions.explanation: The text states the Egyptian civilisation is known for its monumental arch.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Urban societies were more organised than Neolithic farming societies. 2. Civilisations should be considered superior to other forms of societies. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct; statement 2 is incorrect because the text says they should NOT be considered superior.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding early civilisations: 1. They practised crafts. 2. They engaged in trade and exchange. 3. They formed early forms of state. Which of these are features of urban societies?
questions.explanation: Urban societies practised crafts, engaged in trade, and formed political organisations (early states).

questions.question: Which of the following developments occurred as civilisations began to take shape? 1. Huge buildings were built. 2. The art of writing developed. 3. Science and technology contributed to society. 4. Hunter-gatherers ceased to exist.
questions.explanation: Buildings, writing, and science developed; however, hunter-gatherers continued to exist and interact with civilisations.

questions.question: Regarding the relationship between civilisations and other societies: 1. Hunter-gatherers lived in other parts of the world. 2. Pastoralists maintained relationships with civilisations through interactions. 3. The history of hunter-gatherers is less important than civilisations. Which statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is wrong as their history is equally important.

questions.question: Which of the following were elements of the organised way of life in civilisations? 1. Planned settlements 2. Emergence of a polity 3. Social organisation 4. Unregulated food production
questions.explanation: Settlements, polity, and society were organised, but food production was regulated, not unregulated.

questions.question: Identify the correct statements about urban societies: 1. They adopted complex ways of life. 2. They were less organised than hunter-gatherers. 3. They adopted science and technology. 4. They had social stratification.
questions.explanation: Urban societies were more organised (not less) than hunter-gatherers.

questions.question: Which of the following are listed as important early civilisations? 1. Indus 2. Chinese 3. Mesopotamian 4. Egyptian
questions.explanation: All four are listed as important early civilisations.

questions.question: Consider these statements about the emergence of civilisation: 1. It was an adaptation to environmental contexts. 2. It was an adaptation to cultural contexts. 3. It required planning for large populations. Which are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements accurately reflect the text's description of civilisation.

questions.question: Which of the following were characteristics of the Neolithic communities in South India? 1. Agro-pastoralism 2. Microlithic form of life 3. Monumental arch construction 4. Urban stratification
questions.explanation: South India had Neolithic agro-pastoral communities and Microlithic life.

questions.question: Assertion: The term 'civilisation' is used to distinguish urban societies from early forms of societies. Reason: Urban societies adopted complex ways of life, social stratification, and well-planned cities.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
Assertion is false, but Reason is true
questions.explanation: The complex features of urban societies are exactly why the term 'civilisation' is used to distinguish them.

questions.question: Assertion: Civilisations should not be considered superior to other forms of societies. Reason: Each culture or civilisation had its own unique features.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
Assertion is false, but Reason is true
questions.explanation: The uniqueness of each culture is the reason why no single form of society is considered superior.

questions.question: Assertion: The history of hunter-gatherers is less important than the history of early civilisations. Reason: Hunter-gatherers did not have planned cities or writing.
options: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation
Assertion is false, but Reason is true
Assertion is true, but Reason is false
questions.explanation: The assertion is false because the text explicitly states their history is 'equally important'.

questions.question: Urban societies were less organised than early hunter-gatherer societies.
questions.explanation: Urban societies were more organised than hunter-gatherer societies.

questions.question: The art of writing developed as civilisations began to take shape.
questions.explanation: The text states the art of writing developed as civilisations began to take shape.

questions.question: Pastoralists had no interaction with early civilisations.
questions.explanation: Pastoralists maintained relationships with civilisations through interactions.

questions.question: The Egyptian civilisation is one of the oldest civilisations.
questions.explanation: The text describes the Egyptian civilisation as one of the oldest.

questions.question: Match the following:
questions.explanation: Correct answer is Monumental arch

questions.question: What is the blanket of air that surrounds the Earth called?
questions.explanation: The blanket of air that surrounds the Earth is called the atmosphere.

questions.question: Which gas constitutes the highest percentage of the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up 78% of the atmosphere.

questions.question: What is the percentage of Oxygen in the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Oxygen constitutes 21% of the total composition.

questions.question: Which of the following gases acts as a diluent and is chemically inactive?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen acts as a diluent and is chemically inactive.

questions.question: Which gas protects the earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation?
questions.explanation: Ozone helps in protecting the earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation.

questions.question: What is the Greek meaning of the word 'tropos'?
questions.explanation: The Greek word ‘tropos’ means ‘turn’ or change.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Nitrogen and Oxygen are permanent gases. 2. Their percentages always remain the same. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen (78%) and Oxygen (21%) are permanent gases and their percentages remain the same.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Carbon dioxide absorbs heat. 2. Carbon dioxide keeps the atmosphere warm by insulation and radiation. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: CO2 absorbs heat and keeps the atmosphere warm via insulation and radiation.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding solid particles in the atmosphere: 1. They include pollen grains and smoke. 2. They act as nuclei for water vapour condensation. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Solid particles like pollen and smoke act as nuclei for precipitation formation.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the atmosphere's composition: 1. Water vapour varies between 0 - 0.4%. 2. Water vapour is important for predicting weather phenomena. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Water vapour (0-0.4%) is crucial for predicting weather.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the structure of the atmosphere: 1. It is thick near the earth surface. 2. It thins out until it merges with space. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The atmosphere is thickest at the surface and thins out as it reaches space.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about trace gases: 1. Argon is present at 0.93%. 2. Neon is present at 0.0018%. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Argon is 0.93% and Neon is 0.0018%.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. The troposphere is the lowest layer of the atmosphere. 2. The atmosphere is held by gravitational attraction. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct based on the text.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Helium is present at 0.0005%. 2. Hydrogen is present at 0.00005%. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Helium is 0.0005% and Hydrogen is 0.00005%.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Krypton and Xenon are present in trace. 2. Methane is also present in trace. Which of the statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Krypton, Xenon, and Methane are all listed as trace gases.

questions.question: Assertion: The atmosphere is essential for the survival of all forms of life. Reason: It provides the air required for survival.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Air is essential for life, and the atmosphere provides this air.

questions.question: Assertion: Nitrogen is considered a permanent gas. Reason: Its percentage in the atmosphere always remains the same without any change.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is a permanent gas because its proportion (78%) remains constant.

questions.question: Assertion: Solid particles are necessary for precipitation. Reason: They act as nuclei on which water vapour condense.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Solid particles serve as condensation nuclei for water vapour to form precipitation.

questions.question: The atmosphere is held close to the earth by gravitational attraction.
questions.explanation: Gravitational attraction holds the atmosphere close to the earth.

questions.question: Oxygen was discovered by Daniel Rutherford in 1772 CE.
questions.explanation: Daniel Rutherford discovered Nitrogen; Joseph Priestly discovered Oxygen.

questions.question: The troposphere is the highest layer of the atmosphere.
questions.explanation: The troposphere is the lowest layer.

questions.question: Ozone protects the earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation.
questions.explanation: Ozone filters out harmful UV radiation.

questions.question: Match the gas with its percentage:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen 78%, Oxygen 21%, Argon 0.93%, CO2 0.03%.

questions.question: Match the discovery with the scientist:
questions.explanation: Rutherford discovered Nitrogen and Priestly discovered Oxygen.

questions.question: Match the gas with its function:
questions.explanation: CO2 absorbs heat, Ozone protects from UV, Nitrogen is a diluent, Oxygen is for life.

questions.question: Match the solid particles with their examples:
questions.explanation: All listed items are examples of solid particles in the atmosphere.

questions.question: What is the approximate vertical range of the biosphere measured from the ocean floor to the troposphere?
questions.explanation: The vertical range of the biosphere is approximately 20 km.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a small unit of an ecosystem mentioned in the text?
questions.explanation: The text cites the bark of a tree as an example of the smallest units that can sustain life.

questions.question: The biosphere encompasses which of the following spheres of the Earth?
questions.explanation: The biosphere encompasses the Lithosphere, Hydrosphere, and Atmosphere.

questions.question: What is the term used for the area in which an animal, plant, or micro organism lives?
questions.explanation: The area in which an animal, plant or micro organism lives is called its habitat.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as an example of an ecosystem in the text?
questions.explanation: Cropland, pond, forest, and desert are listed as ecosystems; the stratosphere is a layer of the atmosphere.

questions.question: Most plants and animals live in a narrow section of the biosphere approximately how far above and below the Mean Sea Level (MSL)?
questions.explanation: Most plants and animals live in a very narrow section for about 1 km above and below the MSL.

questions.question: What is the global ecosystem also known as?
questions.explanation: The global ecosystem is referred to as the ecosphere.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Biosphere is the fourth sphere of the Earth. 2. It is a life supporting layer. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct as per the introduction of the text.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding an ecosystem: 1. It is a community where living organisms interact with each other. 2. It includes interactions with non-living environments like soil and air. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: An ecosystem involves interactions between living organisms and their non-living environment.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about biodiversity: 1. It refers to a wide variety of plants and animals. 2. These plants and animals live in a particular habitat. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity is defined as a wide variety of plants and animals living in a particular habitat.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the biosphere: 1. It includes flora and fauna. 2. It sustains life forms including mankind. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The biosphere includes flora and fauna and sustains life forms including mankind.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. Biosphere is made up of different ecosystems. 2. Biosphere is made up of different biomes. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The text states that the biosphere is made up of different ecosystems and biomes.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. All living things are grouped into species. 2. Species are only applicable to animals. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: All living things (large or small) are grouped into species, not just animals.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. The biosphere exists on the earth's surface. 2. It encompasses the Lithosphere and Hydrosphere. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The biosphere exists on the earth's surface and encompasses the Lithosphere, Hydrosphere, and Atmosphere.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. The biosphere range is measured from the ocean floor to the troposphere. 2. The total vertical range is approximately 20 km. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements accurately describe the vertical range of the biosphere.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. A pond is an example of a global ecosystem. 2. A forest is an example of an ecosystem. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A forest is an ecosystem, but a pond is a local ecosystem, not a global one (the ecosphere is the global one).

questions.question: Consider the following statements: 1. The biosphere is the third sphere of the Earth. 2. The biosphere is a life supporting layer. Which of the statements above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The biosphere is the fourth sphere of the Earth, not the third.

questions.question: Assertion: The biosphere is considered a life supporting layer. Reason: It encompasses the Lithosphere, Hydrosphere, and Atmosphere.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The biosphere supports life because it integrates the three essential spheres of the Earth.

questions.question: Assertion: Biodiversity is found in a particular habitat. Reason: Biodiversity refers to a wide variety of plants and animals living in a particular habitat.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The reason correctly defines biodiversity, explaining why it is found in a habitat.

questions.question: Assertion: The vertical range of the biosphere is very narrow. Reason: Most plants and animals live within 1 km above and below the Mean Sea Level.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The reason explains why the effective living zone is considered narrow despite the total 20km range.

questions.question: The biosphere is the third sphere of the Earth.
questions.explanation: The biosphere is the fourth sphere of the Earth.

questions.question: An ecosystem can be as small as the bark of a tree.
questions.explanation: The text states ecosystems range from the smallest units like the bark of a tree.

questions.question: The ecosphere is the same as a pond ecosystem.
questions.explanation: The ecosphere is the global ecosystem, whereas a pond is a specific, smaller ecosystem.

questions.question: Habitat is the term used for a wide variety of plants and animals in an area.
questions.explanation: That definition refers to biodiversity; habitat is the area where an organism lives.

questions.question: Match the terms with their descriptions.
questions.explanation: Correct answer is Variety of plants and animals

questions.question: Match the components of the biosphere.
questions.explanation: Correct answer is Air

questions.question: Match the ecosystem examples.
questions.explanation: Correct answer is Smallest unit

questions.question: Match the measurements.
questions.explanation: Correct answer is Troposphere

questions.question: What is the Latin root word for 'colony' and what does it mean?
